sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, p.6 (rendered image). Printed verbatim: "Q25: Bullets and Numbering is under which menu?" then, indented under it, exactly three options with these labels: "C.  Format Menu" (bold + underlined = key), "D.  File Menu", "E.  Window Menu". Nothing precedes "C." - the next thing on the page is "Q26: For what Ctrl + B keyboard shortcut is used?". The surrounding items (Q22, Q23, Q26) all label A/B/C, and Q24 labels A/B, so the C/D/E run is unique to Q25.
note: The paper itself mislabels the options C/D/E; no options were dropped by the extractor (Q26 begins immediately after "E. Window Menu"). Extraction is faithful - keep the source labelling verbatim.

sourceEvidence: exam noooow.pdf, p.1 (rendered image). Printed verbatim: "Q.2. A permanent memory, which halls data and instruction for start-up the computer and does not erase data after power off." Options: "(A) NETWORK INTERFACE CARD  (B) CPU  (C) RAM  (D) ROM  (E) NONE OF THESE" and "ANS. D".
note: "halls" is a typo in the source paper itself, not an OCR artefact - keep verbatim per the fidelity rule.

sourceEvidence: exam noooow.pdf, p.2 (rendered image). Printed verbatim: "Q.4.USB-type storage device is -" with options "(A) SECONDARY  (B) AXILLARY  (C) TERTIARY  (D) PRIMARY" and "ANS. A".
note: The paper genuinely prints the misspelling "AXILLARY", not "AUXILIARY", so option B is not a synonym of A and the item is not double-keyed; the typo is the source's and stays verbatim.

sourceEvidence: exam noooow.pdf, p.8 (rendered image). Printed verbatim: "Q.23. Data gathering in computer means, they allow to use.........data." - the comma splice, the dangling "they", and the nine-dot ellipsis blank are all exactly as the paper prints them.
note: The stem is garbled in the ORIGINAL paper, not in our extraction; we shipped it character-for-character. Nothing to repair. (Separately, and NOT touched here: the source ANS for Q.23 disagrees with our derived key - that belongs to the key-audit pass.)

correctedOptions.label: A
correctedOptions.text: Nothing is wrong, according to the display. The outputs are in the open state and should show zero output voltage.

correctedOptions.label: B
correctedOptions.text: The circuit is in the READ mode and the outputs, Q0-Q3, should reflect the contents of the memory at that address. The chip is defective; replace the chip.

correctedOptions.label: C
correctedOptions.text: The circuit is in the WRITE mode and should be writing the contents of the selected address to Q0–Q3.

correctedOptions.label: D
correctedOptions.text: The Q0–Q3 lines can be either LOW or HIGH, since the chip is in the tristate mode in which case their level is unpredictable.

sourceEvidence: exam noooow.pdf, p.26 (rendered image). Option C is printed verbatim as: "C. THE CIRCUIT IS IN THE WRITE MODE AND SHOULD BE WRITING THE CONTENTS OF THE SELECTED ADDRESS TO Q0–Q3." - where WRITE carries an overbar (active-low WRITE-bar), matching the overlined WRITE pin label on the RAM 256 X 4 block in figure (a). The page also prints "ANSWER OPTION A", and the stem on p.25 matches what we shipped word for word.
note: The six-space hole in option C was the PDF text layer failing to emit the overlined WRITE token; restored as plain "WRITE" (the overbar cannot be carried in plain text) to parallel option B's "READ mode". Stem and key unchanged.
